calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brewster Fong for Andrea Chung-Smith (Petitioner).

Mr. Fong stated that having the Estate funds held in a foreign bank account was an oversight on his part, that he has provided information, that the money is there, that there are no debts or creditor's, and that everything ($61,000.00) is in his Attorney Trust account. The Court explained that the Estate funds need to be earning interest. The Court will allow the petition and distribution with the requirement that all funds in the Attorney Trust account be returned to an Estate account. The Court also advised Mr. Fong that it will consider if there are to be any consequences for the mistakes that were made. The Amended First and Final Report of Executor in Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Continued To Mar-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brewster Fong, for petitioner Andrea Chung-Smith.

The Court stated that proper authentication of the copy of the will was required under the Probate Code, and since the will was executed in Canada and Canada was not a signatory to the 1961 Hague Convention, the petitioner was required to comply with Evidence Code section 1530 requiring a final statement of authentication by appropriate consular officials. Mr. Fong contended that acceptance of the will in Canada constituted a final determination of its authenticity, but the Court indicated that use of a certified copy not having the appropriate authentication as mandated by the Code would not be sufficient; the petitioner could either proceed to obtain the proper authentication or seek to obtain the original will in Canada and present it instead. Mr. Fong requested a continuance to obtain the required authentication. The matter was continued to January 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
